greeting.speaker: Ruth
greeting.text: There you are. You look like you've had a morning. Come sit down.

choices.text: It has been a strange one.

alright.speaker: Ruth
alright.text: You say that and then you sit down like the world owes you something. What happened?

strange.speaker: Ruth
strange.text: Strange like busy or strange like something's off?

obvious.speaker: Ruth
obvious.text: To me it is. Now sit. I'll get you something warm.

check_in.speaker: Ruth
check_in.text: There was a man in here earlier. Government type. Very polite. You know him?

choices.text: He came by the station too.

came_by.speaker: Ruth
came_by.text: Hmm. Making the rounds then. He seemed very interested in how long folks had been here.

what_want.speaker: Ruth
what_want.text: Said he was doing a community survey. Wanted to know how long people had lived here. How they were getting on.

government.speaker: Ruth
government.text: Oh you know the type. Clean shoes, cheap coffee order. Eyes that don't really look at you.

ruth_feeling.speaker: Ruth
ruth_feeling.text: I didn't care for him. Something about a man who smiles too much.

choices.text: You don't trust many people do you Ruth.

fine.speaker: Ruth
fine.text: Maybe. I've just been around long enough to know when a smile isn't one.

same_feeling.speaker: Ruth
same_feeling.text: Then trust it. Your gut's smarter than your head most days.

trust.speaker: Ruth
trust.text: I trust plenty of people. Just not ones that show up unannounced with clipboards.

farewell.speaker: Ruth
farewell.text: You eat something proper tonight. And you come by if you need anything. I mean that.

greeting_return.speaker: Ruth
greeting_return.text: Bowl's still warm. You know where I am.
